glossary.title: Firmware terms
glossary.body: A quick glossary for this widget:

overview.title: What this widget does
overview.body: Build and flash the Klipper firmware on every control board (MCU) of your printer — from one place, no command line. It shows each MCU’s version and whether it matches the host, lets you configure and build firmware per board, and flashes over Katapult / DFU / SD card.

guided.title: Guided new-board flow
guided.body: A four-step checklist to get a new control board running matching firmware: detect the board, configure & build a profile, add & assign the device, then build & flash and verify it’s in sync with the host. Each step opens the right tab and turns green once the live state satisfies it, so you always know what’s next. Nothing here flashes on its own — it guides you to the existing tools, which keep their own confirms.

status.title: Host & MCU versions
status.body: The host runs Klipper; every MCU must run firmware built from the same version. A ⚠ on a device means its firmware is out of sync with the host — rebuild and reflash it. The Linux host’s own “process MCU” is shown too (Moonraker can’t report its version, so FilaMind tracks it).

toolchain.title: Toolchain badges
toolchain.body: These show which build/flash tools are present on the host: Klipper (the source + build system), Katapult, flashtool, dfu-util, avrdude, can-utils. A ✗ means that tool isn’t installed — you only need the ones your boards use. The “setup” badge flags host issues (sudoers, udev rules) with fix-it hints.

services.title: Services
services.body: Start / stop / restart the host services (Klipper, Moonraker, …). Flashing usually stops Klipper so it releases the serial port, then restarts it afterwards — these controls let you do it by hand if needed.

devices.title: Devices
devices.body: Each registered board, with its build/flash actions. “Build” compiles firmware for the board’s profile; “Flash” writes it; “build & flash” does both. The batch buttons (Build all / Flash all / Flash ready) operate on every device at once. Add or attach boards in the Devices manager.

configure.title: Configure & build firmware
configure.body: Pick a board profile and edit its Klipper build options (the Kconfig menu) — toggle “option docs” to read what each setting does, right there. Then “build profile” compiles the firmware and saves the profile so the exact same build can be reproduced later. This builds the shared profile; you flash it to a specific board from the Status tab.

external.title: External firmware
external.body: Flash a pre-built firmware binary you didn’t compile here — a vendor’s `.bin` (e.g. a Beacon, an ERCF board, a CAN toolhead). Register the file, set its flash method and offset, then flash it like any board (behind the same confirm gate). FilaMind inspects the binary best-effort (detected version / MCU / build config) and can diff two files so you can see what changed.

flash.title: Build → flash safely
flash.body: Flashing touches hardware, so FilaMind guards it: it won’t flash mid-print, it stops/restarts Klipper around the operation, and it confirms before irreversible steps. After a flash the board re-enumerates and its reported version updates so you can confirm success.

klipperFirmware.def: The small program running on each control board that turns the host’s step/dir commands into motion. It must be built from the same Klipper version as the host.

mcu.def: A microcontroller — the chip on a control board. A printer often has several: the mainboard, a toolhead board, the Linux host’s “process MCU”, etc. Each runs its own Klipper firmware.

hostMcuSync.def: The firmware on an MCU must match the host’s Klipper version. If it drifts (e.g. after a host update), you get a “version mismatch” — reflash that MCU to fix it.

katapultDfu.def: Bootloaders that let you flash an MCU over USB or CAN without pulling an SD card. Katapult is the modern Klipper bootloader; DFU is STM32’s built-in one.

flashing.def: Writing new firmware onto an MCU. The board usually needs to be in its bootloader first (Katapult / DFU / a reset).

profile.def: A saved firmware configuration (Kconfig / menuconfig) for a board, so you can rebuild the exact same firmware later.

steps: Check each MCU’s version against the host — a ⚠ badge flags firmware that’s out of sync.
Configure a board’s firmware (Configure →) and save it as a profile, so it rebuilds identically.
Build the firmware for that profile and watch the live log for errors.
Put the board in its bootloader (Katapult / DFU) if needed, then Flash — Klipper is stopped and restarted around it.
After flashing, the board re-enumerates and its version updates here — confirm it now matches the host.
widget.healthOkTitle: host is set up for flashing
widget.outdatedTitle: Running firmware differs from the host's Klipper — build & flash to update
widget.noDevices: No devices yet — add a board and assign it a profile to build & flash it here.
widget.noStatus: No firmware status yet — couldn't reach the backend.
widget.confirmFlashWarning: Keep power stable and don’t unplug — interrupting a flash can brick the board.
widget.confirmBuildFlashWarning: This rebuilds the firmware and writes it to the board — keep power stable until done.
widget.confirmBatchTitle: {label} — this flashes multiple devices in turn.
widget.confirmBatchWarning: Keep power stable until every device finishes — interrupting any flash can brick it.
widget.confirmBeaconWarning: Keep the probe connected and powered — interrupting the flash can brick it.
widget.buildFailedSkipFlash: !! Build did not succeed — skipping flash.

guided.title: 🧭 Guided — flash a new board
guided.allDone: ✓ All set — every registered board is built and in sync with the host.
guided.intro: Four steps to get a new control board running matching firmware. Each opens the right tab and turns green once it's done.
guided.stepDetectWhy: Connect the board (USB / CAN / DFU) and scan so FilaMind can see it.
guided.stepConfigureWhy: Pick the board's Klipper options (Kconfig) and build the firmware once as a reusable profile.
guided.stepAssignWhy: Register the board and give it the profile + how it's flashed.
guided.stepFlashWhy: Flash the firmware (behind a confirm), then check the board's reported version matches the host.

flashConfirm.title: ⚠ Confirm flash
flashConfirm.acknowledge: I understand this writes firmware to the hardware and can brick a board if interrupted.

configEditor.title: Configure firmware
configEditor.optionDocsTitle: Show each option's help text inline
configEditor.renameTitle: Rename this profile (and any built binary + linked devices)
configEditor.duplicateTitle: Save a copy of this profile under the new name
configEditor.lockedTitle: Locked — controlled by Klipper (a prerequisite or another option sets it); not directly editable here

devices.title: Devices manager
devices.intro: Add boards, assign a profile, and configure how each is flashed. You build & flash each device from the Status screen (Configure builds the shared profile).
devices.katapultHint: Board carries a Katapult bootloader — it's rebooted into Katapult before flashing. Uncheck for boards flashed directly.
devices.empty: No saved devices yet — add one below.
devices.linkHint: Link this bootloader identity to a registered device
devices.backupHint: One ZIP covers everything you set up: boards, build profiles, motor mapping, board confirmations, the hardware baseline, flash history and saved tuning runs. Restore puts it all back in one step.
devices.restoredFull: Restored {n} profiles, {data} data files, {runs} tuning runs{extra}.
devices.identifySeeded: Profile '{name}' created with {symbol} preselected — open Configure to finish the build options.

external.title: External firmware
external.intro: Flash a pre-built firmware file as-is (no build). Set how each is flashed, then pick a board.
external.compareNote: Read-only comparison of the properties baked into each file.
external.compiledNote: Compiled into the firmware — read-only. To change these, build a profile in Configure and flash that instead.
external.empty: No external firmware yet — upload a .bin to flash it directly.
external.confirmWarning: This is a foreign binary: a wrong file for this board can leave the MCU unbootable until recovered over Katapult/DFU. Verify the file matches the board.
external.confirmAck: I verified this file matches the target board

tools.klipper: Klipper sources + build system — needed to build firmware.
tools.katapult: Katapult bootloader repo — needed for serial/CAN Katapult flashing.
tools.flashtool: Katapult's flashtool.py — flashes serial & CAN boards.
tools.dfu_util: dfu-util — only for DFU (STM32 ROM bootloader) flashing.
tools.can_utils: Optional — only for manual CAN diagnostics (candump/cansend). CAN flashing does NOT need it. Install: sudo apt install can-utils
